Total Loss Vehicle Release & Liability Waiver Disclaimer

Total Loss Determination
This vehicle has been declared a total loss solely by the insurance carrier. 3J's Auto Body did not make this determination and assumes no responsibility for valuation, settlement, salvage handling, title processing, or claim resolution.

BAR Compliance & Teardown
All inspections, diagnostics, and teardown procedures were performed with proper authorization and in compliance with California Business & Professions Code §9884.9 and applicable Bureau of Automotive Repair regulations. The vehicle may be partially dismantled as a result of damage analysis. Reassembly Is Not Performed On Total Loss Vehicles. Vehicle released AS-IS without warranty or representation.

Transfer of Custody to Carrier or Agent
Upon release to the insurance carrier, salvage vendor, or tow provider acting on the carrier's behalf, custody, possession, and control transfer from 3J's Auto Body. After release, 3J's Auto Body is not responsible for:

• Vehicle condition or safety
• Missing or non-reinstalled parts
• Transport or storage damage
• Theft, fire, vandalism, or environmental exposure
• Salvage processing, resale, or disposal
• Valuation or claim disputes
• Title or ownership transfer
• Post-release costs or obligations

Storage Charges (Notice to Carrier)
Storage charges begin after teardown and documentation are completed in accordance with BAR requirements and applicable law. Storage charges continue to accrue until the vehicle is removed from the premises. The total loss determination does not stop or waive storage charges.

Diagnostic & Teardown Labor
Inspection and damage analysis require labor to evaluate and document the vehicle condition. Diagnostic time and teardown labor remain payable regardless of repair outcome.

Carrier Assumption of Risk & Indemnification
Upon pickup, the insurance carrier assumes full responsibility for the vehicle and agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless 3J's Auto Body from any claims, damages, or expenses, including attorney's fees, arising from post-release handling, transport, storage, salvage processing, or claim disputes.

Personal Property
The carrier and/or vehicle owner assumes responsibility for any contents remaining in the vehicle.

General Release & California Civil Code §1542 Waiver
The undersigned releases 3J's Auto Body from all claims arising after release and expressly waives California Civil Code §1542.

I acknowledge receipt of the Total Loss Vehicle Release & Liability Waiver and understand fully
